prompt: A macro close-up photograph of a bizarre fruit, resembling an ancient, dried orange. The fruit is resting on a weathered, sandy wooden surface. It has been sliced perfectly in half. The scene reveals that the fruit has absolutely no internal pulp or juice. Instead, its outer peel has developed into a vast, intricate network of countless, microscopic, razor-thin concentric layers, resembling an endless, complex fractal maze that forms the outer boundary. The entire interior, occupying 99% of the volume, is a dark, hollow, perfectly empty void. Cinematic lighting highlighting the brittle, dry textures. No text, no graphs, highly detailed, 8k resolution. --ar 16:9
metaphor: An orange where the outer peel expands into countless, complex layers, consuming almost all the volume and leaving the center as a vast, empty void.
